mnemonics: CPU TIME = IC * CPI * CT - 'I See People Counting Clocks' to remember the three factors that determine CPU execution time: Instruction Count, Cycles Per Instruction, and Clock Cycle Time.
keyPoints: CPU execution time is the true measure of computer performance.
Amdahl's Law states that the performance improvement is limited by the fraction of time the enhanced feature is used.

MIPS (Million Instructions Per Second) and MFLOPS (Million Floating-Point Operations Per Second) are flawed performance metrics because they don't account for instruction complexity or different program mixes.
commonMistakes: Assuming a higher clock rate (GHz) always means better performance, ignoring CPI and IC.
Using MIPS to compare processors with different instruction sets (e.g., RISC vs. CISC).
Misapplying Amdahl's Law by overestimating the fraction of time an enhancement will be used.
